title: update

goal.text: Refresh the published claw-kit CLI and the current host plugin install surface after a newer version is detected.

requirements.summary: Refresh claw-kit installation surfaces in a controlled order: confirm the host route, update the global CLI first, update the matching host plugin surface second, then verify both surfaces and report exact status.
acceptanceCriteria: The workflow refreshes both the global `@veewo/claw` CLI and the current host plugin surface instead of only one surface.
The workflow keeps the host route explicit for Codex, OpenCode, or a conservative fallback when the host is unclear.
When inside the `claw-kit` repository, the workflow prefers the maintained install scripts instead of inventing replacement commands.
On a remote Codex machine, the workflow uses the repository marketplace and verifies the cached plugin contains every shared skill.
Codex verification proves that the expected marketplace identity is enabled and that its source manifest and installed cache manifest match the target version.
The workflow detects an enabled stale same-name plugin identity instead of treating a newer unused cache directory as success.
Verification explicitly checks the CLI and the chosen plugin surface before success is reported.
The result reports exact success or failure for each refreshed surface.

tasks.title: Confirm the newer-version context and choose the host route
tasks.detail: Tell the user a newer claw-kit version was detected and that you are refreshing the local install surfaces. Confirm whether the current route is Codex, OpenCode, or a conservative fallback because the host is unclear. Keep the route decision explicit before making install changes.

codex.summary: The current host route is Codex.
nextsteps: Then refresh Codex through the maintained direct development install or the official repository marketplace, depending on whether this is a repository checkout or a remote machine.

opencode.summary: The current host route is OpenCode.
nextsteps: Then refresh the local OpenCode plugin install surface.

conservative.summary: The host route is unclear, so the workflow should choose a supported plugin refresh path conservatively.
nextsteps: Inspect the available repo scripts before choosing the plugin refresh command.
If no supported plugin refresh path is available, stop and report that clearly instead of pretending the plugin updated.

tasks.title: Refresh the CLI first, then refresh the matching host plugin surface
tasks.detail: Update the global `@veewo/claw` CLI first, then refresh the plugin surface that matches the chosen host route. Inside the `claw-kit` repository, prefer the maintained scripts: `npm run install:local-cli`, `npm run install:codex-plugin`, and `npm run install:opencode-plugin`; the Codex development installer must refresh both the `claw-kit-local` marketplace source and its versioned cache. On a remote Codex machine, use `codex plugin marketplace add chanyuenpang/claw-kit --ref main` for first setup or `codex plugin marketplace upgrade claw-kit` for refresh, then reinstall or enable `claw-kit@claw-kit` through the Codex plugin command/UI. If another enabled same-name identity such as `claw-kit@claw-kit-local` points at an older source, disable or uninstall that stale identity before success. Do not treat a one-surface refresh as complete, and do not invent a replacement install path when a supported path exists.

default.summary: The requested install surfaces were refreshed; verify each surface before claiming success.
nextsteps: Verify the CLI with `claw --version` and `npm list -g @veewo/claw --depth=0`.
Verify the matching plugin surface with host-specific checks.
Report exact success or failure per surface.

tasks.title: Verify both surfaces and report exact status
tasks.detail: Always verify after the refresh. For the CLI, run `claw --version` and `npm list -g @veewo/claw --depth=0`. For a Codex repository marketplace install, verify `claw-kit@claw-kit` is enabled, confirm its source manifest version, then inspect the matching cache under `%USERPROFILE%\.codex\plugins\cache\claw-kit\claw-kit\`. For a direct development install, verify `claw-kit@claw-kit-local` is enabled, inspect `%USERPROFILE%\.agents\plugins\claw-kit-local\plugins\claw-kit\.codex-plugin\plugin.json`, then inspect the matching cache under `%USERPROFILE%\.codex\plugins\cache\claw-kit-local\claw-kit\`. The source and cache manifest versions must equal the target version. Check for another enabled same-name plugin identity that points at an older source; a newer unused cache directory is not success. Confirm `skills/planning`, `skills/config`, `skills/update`, and `skills/create-claw-skill` exist in the active source/cache copy. Restart Codex and start a new task before validating the loaded skill locator. For OpenCode, confirm the installed plugin files exist under the configured OpenCode plugin path. Do not claim success if only one surface updated, and do not silently skip verification.

references.why: Maps important source information to the converted claw skill surfaces.

rules: This skill is for installation refresh work, not for planning or editing project workflow state.
A claw-kit update is complete only when both the global CLI and the current host plugin surface were refreshed and verified.
Prefer the current host route when it is known; otherwise choose a supported plugin refresh path conservatively.
Inside the `claw-kit` repository, prefer the maintained install scripts instead of inventing replacement install commands.
On remote Codex machines, use the repository marketplace; do not depend on install-time shared-skill generation.
For third-party Codex installs, the expected active identity is claw-kit@claw-kit; detect enabled stale same-name identities.
A cache directory is only an artifact. Verify active identity, marketplace source manifest, and cache manifest together.
Do not claim success if only one install surface updated.
